description: Golden set for cli/eval-kg-matching.js (docs/VECTOR_DB_IMPROVEMENTS.md technique #6). Each case is {mention, expected, tags[, comment]}. `expected` is a case-insensitive substring of the target public landmark name (one of the 433 `location: `-prefixed entries in ingest/corpus/restricted/experiments/kg-embeddings.cache.json, which is also the `locations` table this repo resolves against -- verified by cross-checking ingest/corpus/restricted/extractions/jewish-budapest.location-candidates.json's public_name values, all 9 of which are present in that cache). `expected: null` is a negative control: the mention must not auto-match or exact-match any candidate. Tags are drawn from [hu, en, de, historical, translation-pair, person-name, negative].

cases.mention: the Great Synagogue on Dohány Street

cases.comment: MEASURED FAILURE (prior live eval): zero lexical token overlap between 'erzsebet bridge' and 'elisabeth bridge' under the current normalizer.
MEASURED FAILURE (prior live eval): 'szabadsag'/'liberty' share no tokens.
MEASURED FAILURE (prior live eval): 'margit'/'margaret' share no tokens.
MEASURED FAILURE (prior live eval): Liberty Bridge's pre-1945 name, honoring Franz Joseph I; no token overlap with the current name.
Public candidate is 'Kossuth Lajos utca 19-21. intézmény Astoria-szálló + üzletek, egykor Zrínyi kávéház' -- 'Astoria' is a substring, no standalone 'Astoria' landmark exists in the candidate set.
A district name (Elizabeth Town/7th district), not the bridge. MUST NOT match Elisabeth Bridge despite sharing the 'Erzsébet/Elisabeth' root.
Explicitly non-Budapest; must not match Hungarian State Opera House.
Real rejected mention from ingest/corpus/restricted/extractions/jewish-budapest.location-candidates.json (reason: explicitly_non_budapest).
Ambiguous bare street name (no house number); only specific addressed buildings on Váci utca exist in the candidate set.
Mined from jewish-budapest.location-candidates.json ('Buda Castle Hill' -> Buda Castle, real extraction mention).
Mined from jewish-budapest.location-candidates.json.
Mined from jewish-budapest.location-candidates.json; word-order variant.
Mined from jewish-budapest.location-candidates.json; singular/plural spelling variant of the same landmark.

MEASURED FAILURE: 'Ofen' is the historical German name for Buda. The real extraction's deterministic resolver mismatched this mention to 'Vajdahunyad Castle' at score 0.2133 (see jewish-budapest.location-candidates.json, name_key 'castle of ofen') -- neither candidate was auto-matched, but the top-ranked one was wrong.
Mined from jewish-budapest.location-candidates.json: the bare street, not the synagogue on it. Already covered by lib/kgLocationResolver.test.js's 'high name similarity without an exact match' case.
Mined from jewish-budapest.location-candidates.json: ambiguous bare street (missing house number), not the specific building at Ráday utca 29.

Real rejected mention (explicitly_non_budapest); Pozsony is the Hungarian name for Bratislava.
The Old-New Synagogue in Prague. Real rejected mention; must not match Dohány Street Synagogue despite both being famous synagogues.

MEASURED FAILURE (predicted): the official Hungarian name for the Parliament building itself; zero lexical token overlap with the English name. A decoy 'Országház utca' (a real, different street) also exists in the candidate set but normalizes distinctly ('orszaghaz street' vs 'orszaghaz'), so no false-positive risk.
MEASURED FAILURE (predicted): common Hungarian name, no lexical overlap with the English name.

MEASURED FAILURE (predicted): colloquial Hungarian short name for the bridge; 'lanchid' is a single compound token the current TYPE_WORDS map does not split/canonicalize. Names the bridge's link to Count István Széchenyi only in the English form.
MEASURED FAILURE (predicted): official Hungarian name of the Holocaust memorial.
MEASURED FAILURE (predicted): common Hungarian short name.

cases.expected: Hospital in the Rock Nuclear Bunker Museum
Rudas Thermal Bath and Swimming Pool

cases.comment: Full official name vs. the shorter candidate name; should already resolve via token-subset similarity, not a translation gap.
MEASURED FAILURE (predicted): common colloquial Hungarian name; 'var' (castle) is not in the current TYPE_WORDS map, so it never canonicalizes to 'castle'.
Liberty Square -- a real, different Budapest square. Shares the 'szabadság/liberty' root with Szabadság híd/Liberty Bridge but must not match it; not itself in the candidate set.
Elizabeth Square -- shares the 'Erzsébet/Elisabeth' root with Erzsébet híd/Elisabeth Bridge but is a different landmark (a square, not the bridge); must not match it.
Margaret Island -- shares the 'Margit/Margaret' root with Margit híd/Margaret Bridge but is a different landmark (an island, not the bridge); not itself in the candidate set.
Only specific addressed buildings on Andrássy út (named for Count Gyula Andrássy) are in the candidate set; the avenue itself isn't a standalone landmark there.
